cases.title: Create and recover a local workspace
cases.given: The Airgap application is installed and network access is denied.
cases.when: The user creates a workspace, adds two people, closes the application, deletes the projection directory, and rebuilds.
cases.then: Both people and their canonical data remain intact, validation passes, and no account or network operation occurs.

cases.title: Edit a profile in Obsidian and reopen Liaison
cases.given: A valid person record contains an unknown namespaced field and custom Markdown section.
cases.when: The user edits a normal note in an external editor and then edits a phone number through Liaison.
cases.then: Both edits survive, the unknown content remains byte-equivalent in meaning, and the projection reflects the final record.

cases.title: Resume after interruption
cases.given: The user is editing a multi-section profile.
cases.when: They switch away, close the window, and reopen the workspace.
cases.then: The draft, completed steps, validation state, and next action are restored without duplicate records.

cases.title: Prioritise a relationship
cases.given: A person has recent interactions and no relationship status.
cases.when: The user marks the relationship VIP, sets a monthly cadence, and records a follow-up reason.
cases.then: The relationship view, dashboard, graph alternative, and CLI show the same status, due date, and source-linked reason.

cases.title: Inspect communication coverage
cases.given: Only the last 90 days of one mailbox were imported.
cases.when: The user views email count and last interaction.
cases.then: The result identifies the mailbox, 90-day range, imported count, source link, and incomplete-history warning rather than claiming a lifetime total.

cases.title: Prepare a meeting briefing
cases.given: The EA can read shared profile fields, interactions, and a private EA overlay but not another member’s overlay.
cases.when: They generate a briefing for tomorrow’s meeting.
cases.then: Every statement links to an authorised source, the EA overlay is included, the unauthorised overlay is neither disclosed nor inferable, and writes remain staged.

cases.title: Share operational context without private notes
cases.given: A contact has a shared operational profile and a principal-private overlay.
cases.when: The EA shares the profile with an event coordinator.
cases.then: Only granted operational fields are materialised; private notes, their count, and their existence are not disclosed.

cases.title: Import employee cohort
cases.given: A CSV contains employees, organisations, locations, departments, teams, cost centres, and one duplicate email.
cases.when: The receptionist maps columns, reviews duplicates, confirms the import, and re-imports the same source.
cases.then: The preview identifies the duplicate, confirmed rows are created or updated once, and the second import creates no duplicate people or memberships.

cases.title: Find every dietary gap before an event
cases.given: An event cohort contains provided, verified-none, pending, stale, declined, unreachable, excluded, unknown, and unresolved attendees.
cases.when: The receptionist opens dietary readiness.
cases.then: Every selected attendee appears in exactly one readiness outcome, category totals reconcile, and unknown is never interpreted as no restriction.

cases.title: Produce least-disclosure catering brief
cases.given: Several attendees have restricted detailed dietary notes and shared operational instructions.
cases.when: A receptionist with catering-export permission creates the brief.
cases.then: The brief contains names or approved identifiers and operational instructions only, records source revisions, and excludes diagnoses and ungranted details.

cases.title: Detect stale brief
cases.given: A catering brief was created and an attendee subsequently updates a relevant requirement.
cases.when: The receptionist reopens the event.
cases.then: The previous brief remains historical evidence and is clearly marked stale with the changed attendee identified for authorised review.

cases.title: Build attendee list from filters
cases.given: People have organisation, location, department, cost centre, and saved-group data.
cases.when: The manager filters for Dublin employees in two cost centres and excludes declined invitees.
cases.then: The active predicates are visible, removable by keyboard, and the resulting cohort can be saved and reproduced.

cases.title: Count recorded participation
cases.given: A person has attended, declined, cancelled, and no-show records across events.
cases.when: The manager requests participation counts for a date range.
cases.then: The report states the included states, links to source events, and does not count cancelled events as attended.

cases.title: Import access events safely
cases.given: A source file contains valid rows, duplicates, malformed timestamps, and unknown badges.
cases.when: The facilities operator maps, previews, and confirms valid rows.
cases.then: Valid unique rows are imported once, malformed rows are quarantined with reasons, and unknown badges remain unresolved.

cases.title: Resolve badge identity
cases.given: One badge could match two people by name.
cases.when: The operator reviews the unresolved mapping.
cases.then: Liaison requires explicit authorised selection, records the mapping provenance, and does not use a fuzzy match automatically.

cases.title: Apply retention without performance scoring
cases.given: Access events exceed the configured retention period.
cases.when: The operator previews and applies retention.
cases.then: Eligible payloads are removed with audit evidence, active legal holds remain, and no productivity, attendance-compliance, or risk score is available.

cases.title: See important dates
cases.given: Family profiles contain birthdays with and without years, anniversaries, and activities.
cases.when: A family member opens the upcoming-dates dashboard.
cases.then: Dates are ordered, unknown birth years do not produce invented ages, and each item opens the source record.

cases.title: Respond to a self-service information request
cases.given: A relative receives a signed request for contact preference and dietary operational instruction.
cases.when: They open it offline, provide only the contact preference, decline the other field, and return a response.
cases.then: The requester imports the selected response with provenance and the declined field remains declined rather than unknown or verified none.

cases.title: Exchange updates through removable media
cases.given: Two Airgap devices share a workspace and have registered keys.
cases.when: Each device creates independent updates and exchanges encrypted operation packs.
cases.then: Compatible changes materialise, conflicting changes create an inspectable conflict, and no network access is attempted.

cases.title: Reorder profile fields without changing data semantics
cases.given: B0 is accepted and an A0 workspace has custom fields and two saved profile layouts.
cases.when: The user reorders tabs and fields by keyboard, exports settings, imports them into a clean profile, and edits a value.
cases.then: Stable layout IDs, order, and visibility round trip without data loss while CLI, export, field IDs, values, and validation semantics remain unchanged.

cases.title: Configure dashboard by keyboard
cases.given: The dashboard contains birthdays, overdue follow-ups, events, and recent imports.
cases.when: The administrator adds, moves, hides, and restores panels without drag input.
cases.then: All actions are keyboard-operable, announced, undoable where practical, and restorable to defaults.

cases.title: Complete core workflow at 200 percent zoom
cases.given: The desktop app uses the comfortable density and 200 percent zoom.
cases.when: The user finds a person, edits a phone number, saves, and creates a reminder using keyboard and screen reader.
cases.then: No action or information is clipped, focus order is logical, errors are announced, and completion is confirmed without colour-only meaning.

cases.title: Use graph functionality without graph interaction
cases.given: A relationship graph contains people, relationships, and event links.
cases.when: The user switches to the semantic tree/table view.
cases.then: They can apply the same filters, inspect every edge, open a profile, and perform every supported action without canvas or pointer interaction.

cases.title: Prove Airgap network absence
cases.given: The Airgap artifact is installed in a network-denied test environment.
cases.when: All CLI and desktop workflows, help pages, error paths, and idle periods are exercised.
cases.then: The artifact performs no DNS, loopback, or remote socket operation and contains no enabled network provider or listener capability.

cases.title: Back up and restore offline
cases.given: A workspace contains profiles, interactions, attachments, and a JSONL stream.
cases.when: The user creates an encrypted backup to removable media and restores it into an isolated directory.
cases.then: Manifest, hashes, schema, records, streams, and attachments verify before activation and the original workspace remains unchanged.

cases.title: Approve and revoke a provider grant
cases.given: An S3-compatible connection is configured but inactive.
cases.when: The administrator grants encrypted backup scope for 90 days, runs a job, and revokes the grant.
cases.then: No data is sent before approval, the job uses only encrypted-backup operations and declared destination, and subsequent jobs are denied after revocation.

cases.title: Reject misleading sync capability
cases.given: A WebDAV provider passes backup and single-writer tests but lacks safe concurrent manifest replacement.
cases.when: The administrator inspects provider capabilities.
cases.then: The UI and CLI label it for backup/single-writer use and do not offer multi-writer synchronisation.

cases.title: Detect corrupted remote backup
cases.given: One encrypted remote object is truncated after upload.
cases.when: The user verifies and attempts an isolated restore.
cases.then: Verification fails with the affected object identified, no current workspace is replaced, and upload completion is not shown as recoverable backup success.

cases.title: Use local API with narrow token
cases.given: A token permits person search but not profile reads or writes.
cases.when: An n8n workflow searches, then attempts a profile read and update.
cases.then: Search succeeds, read and update are denied, and audit records the token scope and rejected operations without logging secrets.

cases.title: Deliver signed webhook idempotently
cases.given: A webhook grant permits one event type to one destination.
cases.when: A delivery is retried and the destination redirects to another host.
cases.then: The retry keeps the idempotency key, the undeclared redirect is rejected, and delivery evidence distinguishes retry from success.

cases.title: Summarise locally with Ollama-compatible model
cases.given: Network access is denied and a local model is configured.
cases.when: The user requests a relationship briefing from granted records.
cases.then: The briefing includes source links, no remote account is required, and no network request occurs.

cases.title: Review AI write proposal
cases.given: An AI tool proposes changing relationship priority and adding a note.
cases.when: The user reviews the proposal.
cases.then: They see each record, field, old value, new value, source, grant, validation result, and consequence and can approve items separately or reject all.

cases.title: Reject out-of-scope AI change
cases.given: A model has read permission for one event cohort and propose permission for reminders only.
cases.when: It proposes changing a dietary requirement outside the cohort.
cases.then: The host rejects the proposal before domain execution and records the attempted scope violation.

cases.title: Implement provider without domain changes
cases.given: The object-store contract and SDK conformance kit are available.
cases.when: A developer adds a MinIO-compatible provider.
cases.then: The provider is implemented entirely through adapter, descriptor, schema, tests, and evidence; no People, Events, or Sharing domain crate imports the MinIO SDK.

cases.title: Deny undeclared plugin capability
cases.given: A plugin manifest requests person-read access but no filesystem, network, or secret capability.
cases.when: The plugin attempts to open a workspace path and contact a remote host.
cases.then: Both operations are denied, the plugin remains unable to obtain a raw database handle, and the denial is audited.

cases.title: Maintain plugin contract compatibility
cases.given: A plugin targets a supported older WIT contract.
cases.when: The host upgrades within its advertised compatibility range.
cases.then: The plugin passes compatibility tests or is rejected before activation with a precise migration requirement.

cases.title: Synchronise selected phone contacts
cases.given: The workspace contains 1,000 people and a saved view of 50 active contacts.
cases.when: The user enables CardDAV for that view.
cases.then: Only the 50 selected contacts appear on the phone, stable UIDs are retained, and unrelated archived contacts remain local.

cases.title: Migrate from Meerkat or Monica
cases.given: A synthetic source export contains people, relationships, notes, activities, reminders, birthdays, and custom data.
cases.when: The migration is previewed and confirmed.
cases.then: The reconciliation report maps imported, transformed, unresolved, and unsupported data; source IDs and provenance remain available; re-import is idempotent.

cases.title: Preview remote AI disclosure
cases.given: A remote AI grant covers selected profile fields for one meeting and expires tomorrow.
cases.when: The user opens the disclosure preview and submits the request.
cases.then: The preview and captured fixture payload match exactly, excluded fields and private overlays are absent, and the grant cannot be reused after expiry.

cases.title: Repair invalid external edit
cases.given: An external editor creates a duplicate person ID and invalid birthday.
cases.when: The administrator runs workspace validation and guided repair.
cases.then: Both defects are identified, repair is previewed, no file is silently deleted, and a backup and audit entry exist before changes are applied.

cases.title: Complete Workplace Review Alpha in the installed Mac app
cases.given: A fresh universal internal review build and deterministic 10,000-person workplace fixture are installed for one trusted workspace owner with network access denied.
cases.when: The receptionist imports the directory, finalizes an event cohort, reconciles every dietary outcome, resolves bounded event follow-up, previews disclosure, generates the internal brief, and delivers CSV and HTML.
cases.then: Counts reconcile exactly, restricted detail is structurally absent, delivery verifies without overwrite, canonical non-sensitive files remain readable, no undeclared network request occurs, and no generic task, relationship allocation, cadence, or attention-weight surface exists.

cases.title: Recover authority and healthy records around failures
cases.given: One workspace has a malformed Person, an active writer, and injected operation crashes before and after the commit decision.
cases.when: A second process opens Health, attempts a mutation, and the first process relaunches.
cases.then: Health reports the malformed file, healthy People remain available, the second mutation receives a typed lock error, pre-commit staging is discarded, and committed work rolls forward without overwriting an external edit.

cases.title: Restore encrypted B0 recovery on a clean installation
cases.given: A B0 workspace contains sealed dietary data and the target Mac has no Liaison Keychain entry.
cases.when: The user restores an encrypted recovery package with its passphrase into an isolated directory.
cases.then: Manifest and ciphertext integrity verify, the workspace opens with authorised values available, a wrong passphrase exposes nothing, and the source workspace remains unchanged.

cases.title: Complete Personal Memory Alpha without regressing B0
cases.given: B0 is accepted and the same local workspace foundation is installed.
cases.when: The user edits a profile, records an interaction, creates and completes a commitment, reviews factual reasons, relaunches, and inspects the canonical files.
cases.then: Personal context and open loops survive interruption in readable or sealed canonical forms, no relationship-strength score appears, and the full B0 event workflow still passes.
